text.title: Microservices Observability Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for shipping microservice observability.

rule.statement: Every service MUST export structured logs (JSON) + RED metrics (rate/errors/duration) + distributed traces via OpenTelemetry OTLP. Missing any pillar fails the gate.
rule.rationale: The three pillars cross-reference each other via trace_id; missing one breaks the correlation story.

rule.statement: Every inbound + outbound HTTP/gRPC call MUST propagate W3C `traceparent` (and `tracestate`) headers. B3 / custom propagators are forbidden unless explicit interop rule.
rule.rationale: W3C is the universal vendor-neutral standard; custom propagators silently lose context at boundaries.

rule.statement: Every log line MUST include `trace_id` and `span_id` fields extracted from the active OTel span (or empty if no span). Logs without trace context are forbidden.
rule.rationale: trace_id correlation across logs/traces/metrics is the entire point of observability; without it logs are useless during incident.

rule.statement: Every HTTP/gRPC endpoint MUST export Rate, Errors, Duration (RED) metrics as a histogram bucketed by status code.
rule.rationale: RED is the minimum SLO-measurable surface; alerts on error rate + p99 latency are derived from it.

rule.statement: Span attributes + log fields MUST NOT contain PII (email, phone, full name, IP, SSN). Use hashed identifiers or omit.
rule.rationale: Observability data flows to third-party SaaS backends; PII triggers GDPR/HIPAA exposure.
